body.title: Implement log rotation policy for operational logs
body.result: rotate_logs.py: auto-rotates logs >10MB in ~/.unseen_university/logs/; wired into day-close-audit Step 7; uucompactclaude double-Enter fix incidental
body.intention: I intend that operational logs never grow past 10MB between day-close cycles without automated pruning.
body.description:
Audit found node_registry.log at 16MB and slow_queries.log at 10MB — manually truncated. Need automated rotation (logrotate or tail-based) so logs don't grow unbounded between day-close runs.

**Affected files:** ~/.unseen_university/logs/ (logrotate config or cron) or lab/claudecode/ (rotation script)
**Design rules:** flat-file rotation only — no new DB dependency
**Scope boundary:** IN — configure rotation for *.log in ~/.unseen_university/logs/; OUT — log content changes, structured logging migration
**Completion criteria:** any log >10MB is automatically truncated/rotated; manual intervention not required between day-close runs
